title: Bonafousia muricata
intro: Bonafousia muricata, also known as the "Coral Berry," is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Bonafousia muricata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Bonafousia muricata include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Bonafousia muricata is classified within the family Apocynaceae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This family also includes other well-known species such as the oleander and the frangipani. Within the Apocynaceae family, Bonafousia muricata is part of the tribe Plumerieae, which consists of plants native to tropical regions.
